          -- Use the fact that the set s is finite and the map is injective.

            -- Use the fact that the supremum of the submodules is independent.

                      -- Use the fact that the map is injective.

  · -- Prove iSup_eq_top
    have h₁ : sSup (SetLike.coe I) = ⊤ := I.sSup_eq_top'
    have h₂ : iSup (fun p : I => p.val c) = ⊤ := by
      -- Use the fact that the supremum of the submodules is the top element to prove the supremum of their evaluations at c is the top element.
      have h₃ : iSup (fun p : I => p.val c) = ⨆ p ∈ (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p c := by
        -- Use the fact that the supremum of the submodules is the top element.
        calc
          iSup (fun p : I => p.val c) = iSup fun p : I => (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) c := by simp
          _ = ⨆ p ∈ (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p c := by
            classical
            -- Use the fact that the supremum of the submodules is the top element.
            rw [iSup_congr (fun p => rfl)]
            <;>
